summary: Closed, unmerged attempt to decompile/add fighter collision-related function func_8007B6EC in ftcoll.c with a header declaration in ftcoll.h. The PR was explicitly marked as superseded by #576, so this record should be treated as historical context rather than accepted final code.
what_changed: Added an inline wrapper inline_fn(Fighter* arg0) returning func_800C0694(arg0).
Added a C implementation for func_8007B6EC(HSD_GObj* fighter_gobj) at the end of src/melee/ft/ftcoll.c.

The new function obtains Fighter* via getFighter(fighter_gobj), clears fp->x2221_flag.bits.b0, checks fp->x1990 and fp->x1994, writes fp->x198C, and conditionally calls func_800C0200(fp, 9) if func_800C0694/inline_fn returns 9.
Added a prototype for func_8007B6EC to src/melee/ft/ftcoll.h, but the diff excerpt shows it missing a trailing semicolon.
smart_moves: Kept the new function localized to ftcoll.c and exposed it through ftcoll.h, matching the nearby collision-related function organization.
Included the target address comment 0x8007B6EC and a decomp.me scratch link, which is useful for later matching archaeology.
Used existing Fighter access patterns such as getFighter(fighter_gobj) and existing fighter fields instead of introducing new abstractions.
decomp_lessons: Superseded PRs can still preserve useful scratch metadata and tentative control-flow structure, but should not be treated as canonical without checking the successor PR.
When adding header declarations, verify syntax carefully; the excerpted ftcoll.h declaration lacks a semicolon, which would break compilation if present as shown.
Temporary matching helpers such as inline_fn may be useful during iteration, but successor/final PRs should be checked to see whether the helper survived or was replaced with a direct call.
Variable names like var_r3 and new_var indicate a matching-focused draft rather than cleaned-up semantic naming.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions retained address-style names: func_8007B6EC, func_800C0694, func_800C0200.
Unidentified or compiler-derived locals used decompiler-style names such as var_r3 and new_var.
Existing project naming for fighter object access used HSD_GObj* fighter_gobj and Fighter* fp.
Unknown struct fields retained offset-like names such as x2221_flag, x1990, x1994, and x198C.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduced a small inline wrapper around func_800C0694, likely to influence inlining or call shape for matching, though the PR context does not prove reviewer intent.
Used explicit s32 casts on fp->x1990 and fp->x1994 and an explicit var_r3 temporary, suggesting an attempt to match register/control-flow behavior.
Assigned fp through fp = (new_var = getFighter(fighter_gobj));, a non-semantic-looking pattern likely intended to reproduce compiler behavior.

review_feedback.text: Two reviews are recorded in the PR counts, but no review comment text is present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Inspect PR #576 for the accepted or replacement implementation of func_8007B6EC.
Check whether the missing semicolon in the ftcoll.h prototype was present in the actual submitted branch or only in the excerpt.
Compare the final func_8007B6EC against this draft to see whether inline_fn, var_r3, and the getFighter assignment pattern were needed for matching.
